articles.title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
articles.description:
The Celtics' front court depth took a hit Friday night.

The post Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic will miss one month due to a broken finger.
Vucevic's injury occurred during a game against the Mavericks on March 6.
Boston's front court depth is significantly affected, impacting team performance in upcoming games.

articles.title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics

Tatum's return boosts Celtics' morale ahead of crucial games.

articles.title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
articles.description:

articles.title: Andris Nelsons out as music director of Boston Symphony at end of 2026-27 season
articles.description:
Boston will have the third vacancy among major U.S. orchestras.

The post Andris Nelsons out as music director of Boston Symphony at end of 2026-27 season appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Andris Nelsons will leave his position as music director after the 2026-27 season.

Residents may experience changes in programming and community engagement following this transition.

articles.title: 2 Boston restaurants to pay  $400K for failure to pay service fees to staff, AGO alleges
articles.description:
The Attorney General's Office alleged that the service fees charged from May 2023 to June 2024 didn't go to service employees. The restaurant owners said it was a point-of-sale system mistake.

The post 2 Boston restaurants to pay $400K for failure to pay service fees to staff, AGO alleges appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Two Boston restaurants will pay $400K for unpaid service fees to staff.
Service fees from May 2023 to June 2024 allegedly misallocated, affecting employees.
The Attorney General's Office emphasizes accountability for fair employee compensation.
